note: Detailed clinical management protocols — the deterministic 'Full protocol' reference shown to providers per agent. AUDITED by independent Opus auditors (factual/label/DOI, clinical soundness, fabrication/currency); recon math + all real DOIs verified, no fabrication. 30 agents locked 2026-06-23.

semaglutide.status: FDA-approved (Wegovy / Ozempic / Rybelsus). Continuous therapy — no cycling; follow-up only.
semaglutide.candidacy: Adults BMI ≥30, or ≥27 with ≥1 weight-related comorbidity. Also: adolescents ≥12 (≥95th percentile); MACE reduction in CVD + obesity; noncirrhotic MASH F2–F3 fibrosis (Wegovy, Aug 2025); oral semaglutide 25 mg daily (Dec 2025).
semaglutide.starting_dose: Wegovy 0.25 mg SC once weekly ×4 weeks (initiation only).
semaglutide.hold_rules: If a dose isn't tolerated, delay escalation 4 weeks at the current/prior dose — don't skip steps. If 2.4 mg not tolerated, drop to 1.7 mg ×4 wk then re-attempt; if still intolerant, discontinue. ≥2 missed weekly doses → consider reinitiate + re-titrate.
ae_serious: Acute kidney injury from volume depletion
management: Nausea/vomiting: small low-fat frequent meals, hydrate, HOLD escalation, ondansetron PRN (off-label adjunct), step down if severe
Constipation: fiber, fluids, activity + osmotic laxative
Suspected pancreatitis: STOP, evaluate; do NOT restart if confirmed
Dehydration/AKI: hold, rehydrate, check renal, reassess diuretics/RAAS/NSAIDs
monitoring: Baseline: wt/BMI, BP/HR, renal, glucose/A1c, meds, pregnancy, MTC/pancreatitis screen, mood
~4 wk & each up-titration: tolerability, hydration, wt, HR/BP, adjust insulin/SU, mood
3 mo: weight response, tolerability, glucose
6 mo + every 3–6 mo: weight trend, labs, GI tolerance, mental health
Discontinue if pregnancy
semaglutide.discontinuation: Chronic disease — substantial regain expected (~⅔ over ~1 yr). No mandated taper; restart lower + re-titrate after ≥2 missed doses.
semaglutide.cycle_rest: Continuous — no cycling or off-period. Chronic therapy: ongoing weekly dosing while indicated and tolerated, with routine follow-up. Substantial weight regain (~⅔ over ~1 yr) expected if stopped.

tirzepatide.status: FDA-approved (Mounjaro / Zepbound). Continuous — no cycling; follow-up only.
tirzepatide.candidacy: Adults BMI ≥30, or ≥27 + ≥1 comorbidity; also moderate–severe OSA with obesity (Dec 2024).
tirzepatide.starting_dose: 2.5 mg SC once weekly ×4 weeks (initiation only).
tirzepatide.titration: 2.5 → 5 → 7.5 → 10 → 12.5 → 15 mg (max); +2.5 mg increments after ≥4 weeks. Maintenance 5/10/15 mg.
tirzepatide.hold_rules: Don't escalate until ≥4 wk and tolerated; for GI intolerance stay at current dose or step down — don't skip increments.
management: Same GI / constipation / hypoglycemia / gallbladder / pancreatitis / AKI approach as semaglutide
Reduced oral-contraceptive efficacy → advise non-oral or barrier method for 4 wk after start and after each dose increase
Suspected pancreatitis: STOP, do not restart if confirmed
monitoring: Baseline (incl. oral-contraceptive review)
~4 wk & each up-titration (reinforce contraception counseling after each increase)
3 mo, 6 mo + every 3–6 mo
Discontinue if pregnancy
tirzepatide.discontinuation: Chronic; SURMOUNT-4 withdrawal showed substantial regain. No mandated taper; re-titrate if restarting.
tirzepatide.cycle_rest: Continuous — no cycling or off-period. Chronic weekly therapy while indicated and tolerated; SURMOUNT-4 withdrawal showed substantial regain after stopping.

liraglutide.status: FDA-approved (Saxenda / Victoza). Once DAILY (not weekly). Continuous — no cycling.
liraglutide.candidacy: Adults BMI ≥30, or ≥27 + ≥1 comorbidity; adolescents 12–17 >60 kg with obesity.
liraglutide.hold_rules: Delay escalation ~1 extra week at current dose if intolerant; if 3.0 mg can't be tolerated, discontinue (efficacy is at 3.0 mg). ≥3 consecutive days missed → re-initiate at 0.6 mg + re-titrate.
management: Same class approach (GI diet/hold, fiber/osmotic for constipation, reduce insulin/SU, evaluate gallbladder, STOP + no restart for pancreatitis, hold/rehydrate/check renal for AKI)
monitoring: Baseline; up-titration is weekly; ~4 wk after reaching 3.0 mg
16-week / 4% rule: if NOT ≥4% baseline weight loss by ~16 wk, unlikely effective — consider discontinuation
6 mo + every 3–6 mo
Discontinue if pregnancy
liraglutide.discontinuation: Chronic; regain expected (SCALE Maintenance). Restart at 0.6 mg after ≥3 missed days.
sources: FDA Saxenda label (note 16-wk/4% rule, weekly up-titration)
liraglutide.cycle_rest: Continuous — DAILY dosing, no cycling. Chronic therapy; the 16-week / 4% rule guides whether to continue. Regain expected if stopped (SCALE Maintenance).

retatrutide.status: ⚠ INVESTIGATIONAL — NOT FDA-approved (Phase 3). No label, indication, or approved dose. Do not prescribe outside an authorized trial/IND. All parameters are Phase-2-derived and flagged VERIFY.
retatrutide.candidacy: VERIFY — no approved indication. Phase 2: BMI ≥30, or 27–<30 + ≥1 weight-related condition.
retatrutide.starting_dose: VERIFY — Phase 2 used 2 mg or 4 mg SC weekly; the lower 2 mg start mitigated GI AEs.
retatrutide.titration: VERIFY — weekly SC, stepwise toward 1/4/8/12 mg; max studied 12 mg.
retatrutide.hold_rules: VERIFY — lower start + slower escalation reduced GI events; stay/slow for intolerance, don't skip steps.
ae_common: GI events, dose-related, mostly mild–moderate (per-symptom rates are dose-stratified — VERIFY, not a single number)
Dose-dependent heart-rate increase peaking ~wk 24, then declining
ae_serious: By class, pending Phase-3/label (VERIFY): pancreatitis, gallbladder, AKI, hypoglycemia with insulin/SU, retinopathy, MTC/thyroid C-cell
management: By class analogy (VERIFY): GI diet/hydrate/slow-or-hold + antiemetic; constipation fiber/osmotic; reduce insulin/SU at start; MONITOR HR; gallbladder evaluate; STOP + no restart for confirmed pancreatitis; hold/hydrate/check renal for AKI; follow the trial safety plan
monitoring: Standard GLP-1 continuous cadence (baseline, ~4 wk, 3 mo, 6 mo, ongoing): wt/BMI, GI tolerance, BP, HR (emphasized), renal, glucose, mood + protocol-specific labs/ECG — VERIFY
retatrutide.discontinuation: VERIFY — long-term withdrawal data not yet established; regain expected by class analogy.
retatrutide.cycle_rest: ⚠ VERIFY — investigational, not for use outside an authorized trial. By GLP-1 class analogy it would be continuous (no cycling); long-term withdrawal data not yet established.

sermorelin.status: ⚠ VERIFY — compounded/off-label, NOT FDA-approved. Brand Geref (GHRH 1-29) was approved for pediatric GHD but is discontinued; adult GH-axis use is compounded, off-label, no modern efficacy RCTs. Cycled (not continuous like a GLP-1).
sermorelin.candidacy: Symptomatic adults with low-normal/age-appropriate-low IGF-1 seeking GH-axis support (recovery, body composition, sleep). Empiric — benefit inferred from GHRH-analog mechanism, not proven; requires intact pituitary to respond. Treat hypothyroidism first (gating); exclude active malignancy (absolute).
sermorelin.starting_dose: 300 mcg SC typical (range 200–500 mcg). Recon DEFAULT: 5 mg vial + 5 mL BAC → 1 mg/mL (1,000 mcg/mL) → 300 mcg = 0.30 mL = 30 units (U-100). Units are calculator defaults — recompute from the pharmacy's actual vial mg + BAC mL.
sermorelin.titration: Titrate to lowest effective dose within 200–500 mcg, guided by IGF-1 (target upper-normal-for-age, not above) and symptom/tolerance response. Hold/step down if IGF-1 flat or AEs emerge.
sermorelin.frequency: Nightly at bedtime, fasted (≥2–3 h after the last meal — glucose/somatostatin blunt the GH pulse). Some protocols use 5-on / 2-off weekly dosing.
sermorelin.cycle_rest: Cycle 12–24 weeks, then reassess. No fixed washout — effectively continuous-with-reassessment; titrate to lowest effective dose, hold or stop if IGF-1 is flat or symptoms have resolved.
ae_serious: Theoretical concern with occult malignancy (GH/IGF-1 mitogenic axis — active cancer is an absolute screen-out)
management: Injection-site reactions → rotate SC sites, correct technique, cold compress; usually self-limited
Fluid retention / arthralgia / paresthesia → reduce to lowest effective dose; hold if carpal-tunnel symptoms appear
Glucose drift → reinforce diet, recheck fasting glucose/A1c; reduce or stop if worsening
Treat hypothyroidism first (blunts GH response); reduce concurrent glucocorticoids if feasible
monitoring: Baseline (gating): IGF-1 and TSH/free T4 (treat hypothyroidism first), fasting glucose/A1c, CMP, lipids; exclude active malignancy
Wk 2: tolerance / fluid retention
Mo 1: symptoms + glucose
Mo 3: IGF-1 + glucose (titrate to upper-normal IGF-1)
Mo 6: IGF-1 + body composition (lowest effective dose)
sermorelin.discontinuation: Stop if IGF-1 fails to rise / symptoms unimproved after an adequate course, if IGF-1 exceeds upper-normal-for-age, if carpal tunnel / significant edema / glucose intolerance develops, on hypersensitivity, on pregnancy, or if malignancy is diagnosed. No taper required (secretagogue, not exogenous GH).

tesamorelin.status: FDA-APPROVED (2010, EGRIFTA / EGRIFTA SV) for reduction of excess abdominal visceral adipose tissue (VAT) in HIV-associated lipodystrophy. Continuous per label (effect regresses on discontinuation). Anti-aging / non-HIV use is OFF-LABEL / empiric.
tesamorelin.candidacy: ON-LABEL: HIV-associated lipodystrophy with excess VAT. OFF-LABEL (empiric): non-HIV central/visceral adiposity, GH-axis support — disclose off-label status. Contraindicated if pituitary axis disrupted, active malignancy, or pregnant.
tesamorelin.starting_dose: LABEL fixed dose, no titration. EGRIFTA SV: 1.4 mg SC once daily — reconstitute with 0.5 mL Sterile Water, inject 0.35 mL. Original EGRIFTA: 2 mg SC once daily — reconstitute with 2.1 mL Sterile Water, inject 2 mL. Use STERILE WATER, NOT BAC. EGRIFTA SV ≠ original EGRIFTA (different strength/recon — do not conflate).
tesamorelin.titration: Fixed dose — no titration. Manage by continue / discontinue, not up-titration.
tesamorelin.frequency: Once daily SC, abdomen, rotate injection sites.
tesamorelin.cycle_rest: Continuous per label — no scheduled off-period. Off-label VAT protocols reassess at 12–26 wk and discontinue if no VAT response.
ae_common: Injection-site reactions (erythema, pruritus, pain, irritation, hemorrhage, hypertrophy)
ae_serious: Glucose intolerance / new or worsening diabetes
Acute critical illness (GH-class mortality signal — consider discontinuing)
management: Injection-site reactions → rotate abdominal sites, technique review; usually self-limited
Fluid retention / arthralgia / carpal tunnel → manage symptomatically; discontinue if persistent/severe
Glucose intolerance → monitor glucose/A1c; manage diabetes; discontinue if worsening
Intercurrent acute critical illness → consider holding/discontinuing
monitoring: Baseline: IGF-1, fasting glucose/A1c, exclude active malignancy and pituitary-axis disruption, pregnancy status
On therapy: periodic IGF-1 (consider discontinuation with persistent elevation), glucose/A1c, edema / carpal-tunnel symptoms, VAT/clinical response
Malignancy surveillance; discontinue on any recurrence
tesamorelin.discontinuation: Discontinue for persistent IGF-1 elevation, malignancy or recurrence, pregnancy, hypersensitivity, intolerable fluid retention/CTS, uncontrolled glucose intolerance, acute critical illness, or (off-label) no VAT response by 12–26 wk. No taper. VAT benefit regresses after stopping.
sources: FDA LABEL (EGRIFTA / EGRIFTA SV prescribing information)
Falutz 2010, JCEM — ~15% VAT reduction vs placebo, glucose-neutral (doi:10.1210/jc.2010-0490)

cjc-1295.status: ⚠ VERIFY — compounded/research-only, NOT FDA-approved. Default here is no-DAC (mod GRF 1-29), dosed DAILY. The DAC ester is a different molecule on a weekly schedule (1–2 mg/wk) — do not conflate. Cycled.
cjc-1295.candidacy: Symptomatic adults with low-normal IGF-1 seeking GH-axis support (recovery, body composition); almost always paired with a ghrelin agonist (ipamorelin). Empiric — the only human RCT is of the DAC form (PK/PD); no-DAC has no efficacy RCT. Treat hypothyroidism first; exclude active malignancy (absolute).
cjc-1295.starting_dose: 100 mcg SC typical (range 100–200 mcg), no-DAC. Recon DEFAULT: 5 mg vial + 2.5 mL BAC → 2 mg/mL (2,000 mcg/mL) → 100 mcg = 0.05 mL = 5 units (U-100). Recompute from actual fill.
cjc-1295.titration: Titrate within 100–200 mcg to upper-normal IGF-1, lowest effective dose. Watch fluid retention at higher doses.
cjc-1295.frequency: Nightly at bedtime, fasted (no-DAC daily schedule).
cjc-1295.cycle_rest: Cycle 12–16 weeks, then reassess. Continuous-with-reassessment — titrate to upper-normal IGF-1, lowest effective dose; hold/stop on plateau or AEs.
ae_serious: Glucose intolerance / new or worsening diabetes
Occult-malignancy concern (mitogenic GH/IGF-1 axis — active cancer is an absolute screen-out)
management: Site reactions → rotate, technique, cold compress
Fluid retention / arthralgia / paresthesia → reduce to lowest effective dose; hold if CTS develops
Glucose drift → reinforce diet, recheck glucose/A1c; reduce/stop if worsening
Treat hypothyroidism first (gating); reinforce bedtime-fasted timing
monitoring: Baseline (gating): IGF-1 and TSH/free T4 (treat thyroid first), fasting glucose, CMP, lipids; cancer screen current
Wk 2: tolerance / fluid retention (reinforce timing)
Mo 1: symptoms + glucose
Mo 3: IGF-1 + glucose (titrate to upper-normal IGF-1)
Mo 6: IGF-1 + composition (lowest effective dose)
cjc-1295.discontinuation: Stop on IGF-1 plateau without benefit, IGF-1 above upper-normal, CTS / significant edema / glucose intolerance, hypersensitivity, pregnancy, or malignancy diagnosis. No taper (secretagogue).
sources: Teichman 2006, JCEM (human DAC RCT; t½ ~6–8 d; doi:10.1210/jc.2005-1536)

ipamorelin.status: ⚠ VERIFY — compounded/research-only, NOT FDA-approved. Two completed human Phase 2 trials (GI motility) were both negative; zero human efficacy data for anti-aging/body-composition use. Cycled.
ipamorelin.candidacy: Symptomatic adults with low-normal IGF-1 seeking GH-axis support (recovery, body composition, sleep), typically paired with a GHRH analog (CJC-1295 or sermorelin). Empiric — 'cleanest' GHRP (minimal cortisol/prolactin/hunger vs GHRP-6) but no proven efficacy. Treat hypothyroidism first; exclude active malignancy (absolute).
ipamorelin.starting_dose: 200–300 mcg SC (typical 300 mcg). Recon DEFAULT: 5 mg vial + 2.5 mL BAC → 2 mg/mL (2,000 mcg/mL) → 300 mcg = 0.15 mL = 15 units (U-100).
ipamorelin.titration: Titrate within 200–300 mcg to lowest effective dose; guided by IGF-1 (upper-normal target) and tolerance. (Some protocols dose BID.)
ipamorelin.cycle_rest: Cycle 12–16 weeks, then reassess. Continuous-with-reassessment; lowest effective dose; hold/stop on plateau or AEs.
ae_common: Somnolence / vivid dreams (minimal appetite/cortisol/prolactin effect vs other GHRPs)
ae_serious: Glucose intolerance / new or worsening diabetes
Occult-malignancy concern (active cancer is an absolute screen-out)
management: Site reactions → rotate, technique, cold compress
Fluid retention / arthralgia / paresthesia → reduce to lowest effective; hold if CTS appears
Glucose drift → diet reinforcement, recheck glucose/A1c; reduce/stop if worsening
Treat hypothyroidism first (gating); reinforce bedtime-fasted timing
monitoring: Baseline (gating): IGF-1 and TSH/free T4, fasting glucose, CMP, lipids; cancer screen current
Wk 2: tolerance / sleep (reinforce timing)
Mo 1: symptoms + glucose
Mo 3: IGF-1 + glucose (titrate to upper-normal IGF-1)
Mo 6: IGF-1 + composition (lowest effective dose)
ipamorelin.discontinuation: Stop on plateau without benefit, IGF-1 above upper-normal, CTS / edema / glucose intolerance, hypersensitivity, pregnancy, or malignancy. No taper (secretagogue).
sources: Beck 2014, Int J Colorectal Dis — negative human Ph2 (GI motility)

bpc-157.status: ⚠ VERIFY — research-only, NOT FDA-approved. FDA placed BPC-157 in Category 2 of the interim 503A bulk-substances list (significant-safety-concern); as of Apr 15, 2026 FDA REMOVED it from Category 2 — it is NOT yet on the 503A positive bulks list, and a PCAC review is scheduled for Jul 23–24, 2026. Compounding status is unresolved/in-review, not a flat ban — VERIFY current status before compounding. Cycled — short goal-bound courses.
bpc-157.candidacy: Adjunct for refractory soft-tissue (tendon/ligament/muscle) injury or GI mucosal complaints that have plateaued on standard care. Empiric — animal data only, no human efficacy proof. Image the injury first — do not use it to dodge a surgical lesion. Active or prior malignancy is an ABSOLUTE contraindication (pro-angiogenic).
bpc-157.starting_dose: 250 mcg SC daily (typical); range 250–500 mcg/day. Recon DEFAULT: 5 mg vial + 2 mL BAC → 2.5 mg/mL (2,500 mcg/mL) → 250 mcg = 0.10 mL = 10 units (U-100). SC, local to injury preferred; oral variants exist (community-used for GI complaints; SC is the protocol standard).
bpc-157.titration: Escalate to 500 mcg or stop if no signal by ~Wk 2; may split BID at the higher end. Otherwise fixed within range.
bpc-157.frequency: Once daily (may split BID at the higher end).
bpc-157.cycle_rest: Cycle 4–6 weeks, then reassess and stop. Off after the course (NOT continuous); re-course only if goal-bound and re-screened.
ae_common: Note: human safety data are essentially absent — AE profile is poorly characterized
ae_serious: Theoretical tumor promotion (pro-angiogenic VEGFR2 mechanism — absolute screen-out for active/prior malignancy)
Risk of masking an undiagnosed surgical/malignant lesion
Unknown long-term human safety and source/purity hazard (unregulated supply)
management: Site reactions / GI upset → rotate sites, dose timing; usually self-limited
Any new lump, unexplained weight loss, or red-flag symptom → STOP and work up malignancy
Keep courses short and goal-bound; if injury unresolved by ~Mo 3, pivot to definitive care (PT/surgery), re-exam ± image
Verify source pharmacy; counsel on unregulated supply
monitoring: Baseline: CBC, CMP; age-appropriate cancer screening current (gating)
Wk 2: tolerance / site / response (escalate to 500 mcg or stop if no signal)
Mo 1: functional outcome (ROM/pain) — continue short course or stop
Mo 3: should be off, re-exam ± image
Mo 6: malignancy ROS — stop + work up any red flag
bpc-157.discontinuation: Stop at end of the 4–6 wk course (default), on any malignancy red flag, on hypersensitivity, on pregnancy, or if functional goal unmet — pivot to definitive care. No taper.
sources: Compounding: removed from 503A Category 2 Apr 15 2026; PCAC review Jul 23–24 2026 — VERIFY

tb-500.status: ⚠ VERIFY — research-only, NOT FDA-approved. The consumer fragment 'TB-500' has no completed human efficacy trial — do NOT conflate with full-length Tβ4 (RGN-259), a separate molecule with completed Phase 3 ophthalmic RCTs. Cycled.
tb-500.candidacy: Adjunct for refractory soft-tissue/tendon/ligament/muscle injury (often stacked with BPC-157). Empiric; published human data are doping-analytics only, no efficacy. Active or prior malignancy is an ABSOLUTE contraindication (actin-binding, pro-angiogenic/pro-migratory).
tb-500.starting_dose: 2–2.5 mg SC twice weekly during loading, then 2 mg SC once weekly maintenance. Recon DEFAULT: 10 mg vial + 2 mL BAC → 5 mg/mL (5,000 mcg/mL) → 2 mg = 0.40 mL = 40 units (U-100).
tb-500.titration: Fixed loading/maintenance convention — no fine titration. Step down from loading (2×/wk) to maintenance (1×/wk) per the standard schedule.
tb-500.cycle_rest: Cycle ~6 weeks loading + 4–6 weeks maintenance. Off after maintenance (NOT continuous); re-course only if re-screened.
ae_common: Human safety data essentially absent — AE profile poorly characterized
ae_serious: Theoretical tumor promotion / unwanted cell migration / fibrosis (actin-binding, pro-angiogenic/pro-migratory — absolute screen-out for active/prior malignancy; caution in proliferative retinopathy)
management: Site reactions / fatigue → rotate sites, adjust timing; usually self-limited
Any new lump, weight loss, or red-flag symptom → STOP and work up malignancy
Caution/avoid in proliferative retinopathy or settings where fibrosis/migration could harm
Verify source pharmacy; counsel that the fragment is not RGN-259
monitoring: Baseline: CBC, CMP; age-appropriate cancer screening current (gating); retinal status if at risk
Tolerance / site each phase
Functional outcome at end of loading
Reassess at end of maintenance; malignancy ROS at follow-up; stop on any red flag
tb-500.discontinuation: Stop at end of the loading + maintenance course (default), on any malignancy red flag, on hypersensitivity, on pregnancy, or if functional goal unmet — pivot to definitive care. No taper.
sources: Full-length Tβ4/RGN-259 is a distinct molecule — do not conflate

cjc-ipamorelin-blend.status: ⚠ VERIFY — investigational, NOT FDA-approved (compounded GHRH-analog + ghrelin-agonist combo; no human trials of the combination exist). Cycled. Grade D.
cjc-ipamorelin-blend.candidacy: Symptomatic adults with low-normal IGF-1 for recovery, body composition, sleep. Gating: treat hypothyroidism first, current cancer screening. Absolute: active malignancy; pregnancy/lactation/age <18; hypersensitivity to either component; non-functional/disrupted pituitary axis (extrapolated, VERIFY).
cjc-ipamorelin-blend.starting_dose: ~100 mcg CJC-1295 + 200–300 mcg ipamorelin SC per nightly dose. Recon DEFAULT: 5 mg CJC + 5 mg ipa co-vialed + 2.5 mL BAC = 2 mg/mL each → 0.15 mL = 15 units (U-100, ≈300 mcg each).
cjc-ipamorelin-blend.titration: Titrate to upper-normal-for-age IGF-1 (lowest effective dose); watch fluid retention / glucose.
cjc-ipamorelin-blend.frequency: One SC injection nightly at bedtime, fasted.
cjc-ipamorelin-blend.cycle_rest: 12–16 weeks, then reassess. Continuous-with-reassessment — no fixed washout; hold/step down or stop if IGF-1 flat / no benefit.
ae_serious: Sustained IGF-1 elevation above upper-normal (proliferative/malignancy concern)
management: Manage glucose; titrate down to lowest effective dose
monitoring: Baseline (gating): IGF-1, TSH/free T4; fasting glucose, CMP, lipids
Wk 2: tolerance / fluid
Mo 1: symptoms / glucose
Mo 3: IGF-1 + glucose (titrate to upper-normal)
Mo 6: IGF-1 + composition
cjc-ipamorelin-blend.discontinuation: Stop for active malignancy / cancer red flag, pregnancy, persistent IGF-1 elevation, significant edema / CHF signal, intolerable arthralgia / carpal tunnel, hypersensitivity, or no benefit. Effects regress off-drug.
sources: Coutinho 2026, J Sports Med Phys Fitness (doi:10.23736/S0022-4707.26.17773-1)

ghrp-2.status: ⚠ VERIFY — research-only, NO US therapeutic approval. GH-stimulation diagnostic agent abroad; no efficacy trials for anti-aging/body-composition. No validated cycle. Grade D.
ghrp-2.candidacy: No validated therapeutic candidacy — reference entry. Absolute: active malignancy; pregnancy/lactation/age <18; hypersensitivity. Relative: diabetes/insulin resistance; untreated/severe hypothyroidism; cortisol & prolactin elevation; appetite stimulation; fluid retention/edema/carpal tunnel; OSA; retinopathy; acute critical illness.
ghrp-2.starting_dose: Reported ~100 mcg SC (range 100–300 mcg). Recon DEFAULT: 5 mg + 2.5 mL BAC = 2 mg/mL → 100 mcg = 0.05 mL = 5 units.
ghrp-2.titration: No validated titration; empiric to upper-normal IGF-1. VERIFY.
ghrp-2.frequency: 1–3× daily (AM fasted / pre-bed / post-workout); bedtime-fasted leverages the GH pulse.
ghrp-2.cycle_rest: No validated protocol (community 8–12 wk reported). No validated washout.
management: Reduce/hold for edema or glucose worsening
monitoring: Baseline: IGF-1, TSH/free T4, fasting glucose/A1c; consider prolactin/cortisol
On-treatment: glucose/A1c, IGF-1 (upper-normal), edema/appetite
ghrp-2.discontinuation: Active malignancy / cancer red flag, pregnancy, hypersensitivity, persistent IGF-1 elevation, edema, endocrine derangement, or no benefit.
sources: No DOI citations in the curated library (citations: []). Diagnostic agent abroad; 2024–26 literature diagnostic only. VERIFY — no fabricated reference.

ghrp-6.status: ⚠ VERIFY — research-only, NOT FDA-approved; no human efficacy trials for wellness. 2024–26 literature preclinical only. No validated cycle. Grade D.
ghrp-6.candidacy: No validated therapeutic candidacy — reference entry. Defining feature: marked appetite stimulation (strongest GHRP) — counsel weight gain. Absolute: active malignancy; pregnancy/lactation/age <18; hypersensitivity. Relative: appetite stimulation; diabetes/insulin resistance; cortisol & prolactin elevation; untreated/severe hypothyroidism; edema/carpal tunnel; OSA; retinopathy; acute critical illness.
ghrp-6.starting_dose: Reported ~100 mcg SC. Recon DEFAULT: 5 mg + 2.5 mL BAC = 2 mg/mL → 100 mcg = 0.05 mL = 5 units.
ghrp-6.cycle_rest: No validated protocol. No validated washout.
monitoring: Baseline: IGF-1, TSH/free T4, fasting glucose/A1c; weight/appetite each visit
Glucose/A1c; IGF-1 (upper-normal)
ghrp-6.discontinuation: Active malignancy / cancer red flag, pregnancy, hypersensitivity, unmanageable appetite/weight, glucose worsening, persistent IGF-1 elevation, or no benefit.
sources: No DOI citations in the curated library (citations: []). No human efficacy trials; 2024–26 preclinical. VERIFY — no fabricated citation.

hexarelin.status: ⚠ VERIFY — research-only, NO human therapeutic approval or efficacy trials; most potent GHRP but prone to receptor desensitization (tachyphylaxis). 2024–26 literature preclinical/analytical (cardiac CD36 binding). No validated cycle. Grade D.
hexarelin.candidacy: No validated therapeutic candidacy — reference entry. Receptor-desensitization caution is defining: dose short/intermittent. GATE on baseline cardiac history/symptoms (named CD36 cardiac-binding mechanism) — caution in cardiac disease. Absolute: active malignancy; pregnancy/lactation/age <18; hypersensitivity. Relative: receptor desensitization/tachyphylaxis; cortisol & prolactin elevation; diabetes/insulin resistance; untreated/severe hypothyroidism; cardiac CD36-binding (preclinical); edema/carpal tunnel; OSA; retinopathy; acute critical illness.
hexarelin.starting_dose: Reported ~100 mcg SC. Recon DEFAULT: 5 mg + 2.5 mL BAC = 2 mg/mL → 100 mcg = 0.05 mL = 5 units.
hexarelin.titration: No validated titration; keep exposure low/intermittent to limit desensitization. VERIFY.
hexarelin.frequency: 1–2× daily (kept lower than other GHRPs because of tachyphylaxis); bedtime/fasted.
hexarelin.cycle_rest: No validated protocol; deliberately short / intermittent due to desensitization. Off-periods advised to limit GHSR desensitization (no validated schedule).
management: Short, intermittent courses with off-periods to preserve receptor sensitivity
Reduce/hold for cardiac symptoms / edema / glucose; cardiac symptoms during use → hold/stop
monitoring: Baseline: IGF-1, TSH/free T4, fasting glucose/A1c; cardiac history (CD36) — gate
On-treatment: glucose/A1c, IGF-1 (upper-normal), watch for waning response and cardiac symptoms
hexarelin.discontinuation: Active malignancy / cancer red flag, pregnancy, hypersensitivity, cardiac symptoms, glucose worsening, loss of response (desensitization), or no benefit.
sources: No DOI citations in the curated library (citations: []). No approval/efficacy trials; 2024–26 preclinical/analytical. VERIFY — no fabricated citation.

mk-677.status: ⚠ VERIFY — research-only, NO FDA approval; orally active non-peptide ghrelin/GHS-R agonist raising IGF-1. ORAL — no reconstitution. Community cycling reported; no validated protocol. Grade C/D.
mk-677.candidacy: Adults wanting sustained GH/IGF-1 elevation (~24 h half-life) preferring oral dosing. Documented liability: reproducibly raises fasting glucose, reduces insulin sensitivity. Absolute: active or prior malignancy; pregnancy/lactation/age <18; hypersensitivity. Relative: CHF / fluid-retention — avoid in HF; diabetes/insulin resistance (documented — monitor); increased appetite; untreated/severe hypothyroidism; OSA; retinopathy; elevated IGF-1 (long-term safety unknown); acute critical illness; atraumatic splenic-rupture case report (with RAD-140).
mk-677.starting_dose: 10–25 mg orally once daily (reported typical). Reconstitution: oral — n/a.
mk-677.titration: No validated titration; empiric within 10–25 mg/day to upper-normal IGF-1, watching glucose/edema. VERIFY.
mk-677.frequency: Once daily, usually at night (~24 h half-life; bedtime favored for sleep/GH-pulse).
mk-677.cycle_rest: Community cycling reported; no validated protocol. No validated washout.
ae_common: Increased fasting glucose / reduced insulin sensitivity (documented)
ae_serious: Worsening glucose intolerance / new or worsened diabetes (documented)
CHF / heart-failure signal in older adults — avoid in HF
Atraumatic splenic rupture (case report w/ RAD-140)
management: Monitor glucose/A1c and edema closely; avoid in HF
Dose-reduce/hold for edema / glucose worsening / CHF signs
Lowest effective dose keeping IGF-1 upper-normal
monitoring: Baseline: IGF-1, TSH/free T4, fasting glucose/A1c, CMP, lipids; weight/edema/BP
Priority on-treatment checks: glucose/A1c and edema; IGF-1 upper-normal; periodic reassessment
mk-677.discontinuation: Active/prior malignancy or cancer red flag, pregnancy, CHF / significant edema, worsening diabetes, persistent IGF-1 elevation, hypersensitivity, or no benefit.
sources: Jaffry 2026, Cureus (atraumatic splenic-rupture case report w/ RAD-140). Glucose/insulin-resistance and edema/CHF effects documented per library. VERIFY; no fabrication.

aod-9604.status: ⚠ VERIFY — investigational, NOT FDA-approved (FDA GRAS food-ingredient only). Phase 2 obesity development discontinued for lack of efficacy; zero registered trials. Cycled. Grade D.
aod-9604.candidacy: Adjunct fat-loss within a structured program — modest at best, not standalone. Clean metabolic profile: no IGF-1 or glucose impact (GH fragment 176-191, lipolytic). Absolute: hypersensitivity; pregnancy/lactation; age <18. Relative: active malignancy (precautionary only — weak rationale, no IGF-1 stimulation); compounded/unregulated sourcing; no established DDI profile.
aod-9604.starting_dose: 300 mcg SC daily; range 250–500 mcg/day. Recon DEFAULT: 5 mg + 2 mL BAC = 2.5 mg/mL → 300 mcg = 0.12 mL = 12 units.
aod-9604.titration: Within 250–500 mcg/day by response — narrow empiric range, not a formal titration. VERIFY.
aod-9604.cycle_rest: 8–12 weeks, reassess. No standardized validated off-period — stop if no objective change by ~6 months.
ae_common: No glucose or IGF-1 effect (distinguishing feature)
monitoring: Baseline: CMP, fasting glucose, lipids, weight/waist
Wk 2: tolerance / site
Mo 1: weight/waist trend
Mo 3: composition / lipids
Mo 6: maintenance — stop if no objective change
aod-9604.discontinuation: Hypersensitivity, pregnancy, or no objective change by ~6 months.
sources: Wilding 2004, Curr Opin Investig Drugs (review, PMID 15134286)

mots-c.status: ⚠ VERIFY — investigational, NOT FDA-approved (mitochondrial-derived peptide; no completed human therapeutic RCT — first Ph2a NCT07505745 recruiting). Cycled. Grade D→C.
mots-c.candidacy: Adults seeking metabolic / insulin-sensitivity support tied to an objective endpoint (HOMA-IR / A1c). Absolute: hypersensitivity; pregnancy/lactation (no human data); age <18. Relative: active malignancy (precautionary; mixed preclinical signals); diabetes / glucose-lowering agents — AMPK/insulin sensitization → theoretical additive hypoglycemia, monitor glucose; compounded/unregulated sourcing; no DDI profile.
mots-c.starting_dose: 5–10 mg SC per dose. Recon DEFAULT: 10 mg + 1 mL BAC = 10 mg/mL → 5 mg = 0.50 mL = 50 units.
mots-c.titration: Within 5–10 mg/dose, empiric to metabolic response. VERIFY.
mots-c.cycle_rest: 4–8 weeks, tied to a metabolic endpoint. Continue only with objective benefit; stop if no durable change — no validated fixed off-period.
ae_common: Theoretical additive glucose lowering with antidiabetics
monitoring: Baseline: fasting glucose/A1c, HOMA-IR, CMP, lipids
Glucose/A1c & HOMA-IR at endpoint; reassess 1–6 months; malignancy precaution
mots-c.discontinuation: Hypersensitivity, pregnancy, unmanageable hypoglycemia, or no durable metabolic change.
sources: Zheng 2023 (DOIs not enumerated in dosing.md — confirm against library; no fabrication)

ss-31.status: FDA-APPROVED as FORZINITY (elamipretide, accelerated approval Sept 2025) to improve muscle strength in Barth syndrome (patients ≥30 kg) — that approval does NOT cover the metabolic/longevity use here, which is OFF-LABEL / empiric. SC only — not IV. Benzyl-alcohol excipient — NOT for neonates. Cycled (off-label use).
ss-31.candidacy: OFF-LABEL longevity use: adults seeking mitochondrial / metabolic-longevity support. Absolute (carried from FORZINITY label): serious hypersensitivity to elamipretide/excipient. Relative: serious allergic reactions; SC only — not IV; benzyl-alcohol — not neonates, avoid pregnancy/neonates; injection-site reactions; pregnancy (limited data — VERIFY); lactation (no data — VERIFY); active malignancy (precautionary, not label — VERIFY); compounded SS-31 vs FORZINITY — purity/benzyl-alcohol/dosing unverified (VERIFY).
ss-31.starting_dose: OFF-LABEL empiric: 5–10 mg SC daily. Recon DEFAULT: 10 mg + 1 mL BAC = 10 mg/mL → 10 mg = 1.0 mL = 100 units; 5 mg = 0.5 mL = 50 units. (FORZINITY LABEL dose for Barth, for reference, is fixed 40 mg SC once daily for ≥30 kg; 20 mg daily in severe renal impairment [eGFR <30, not on dialysis] — NOT weight-titrated. Mito-myopathy trials used 40 mg SC daily.)
ss-31.titration: Empiric within 5–10 mg/day (off-label); no validated titration → VERIFY. Label use is fixed-dose, no titration.
ss-31.cycle_rest: Off-label: 4–8 weeks; reassess 1–6 months; stop if no durable functional benefit. No validated off-period. (Label use for Barth is continuous/chronic.)
ae_serious: Hypersensitivity / serious allergic reaction — discontinue/seek care
monitoring: Baseline + on-treatment functional/metabolic endpoint to justify continuation
Injection-site / hypersensitivity surveillance
Malignancy precaution
ss-31.discontinuation: Serious hypersensitivity / allergic reaction, pregnancy concern (benzyl alcohol), or no durable functional benefit.
sources: FDA LABEL (FORZINITY / elamipretide; Barth syndrome, Sept 2025) — VERIFY exact dosing against the PI
Karaa 2023, Neurology (MMPOWER-3 — missed its co-primary endpoints: 6MWT and fatigue)
Szeto 2014 (DOIs not enumerated — confirm against library)
Off-label dosing = empiric, NOT validated → VERIFY

ghk-cu.status: ⚠ VERIFY — NOT FDA-approved. Strongest evidence is topical/cosmetic; injected/systemic SC use is empiric — no completed RCT. Cycled — short courses then off (NOT continuous), with attention to cumulative copper. A Ph2 topical RCT is recruiting (NCT07437586).
ghk-cu.candidacy: Adults seeking skin/tissue-repair or cosmetic benefit; topical route has the better (still modest) evidence. Empiric for systemic SC. Active or prior malignancy is ABSOLUTE (pro-angiogenic); Wilson disease / copper-overload is ABSOLUTE.
ghk-cu.starting_dose: 1–2 mg SC daily (typical 2 mg) — or topical 1–2% serum. Recon DEFAULT: 50 mg vial + 5 mL BAC → 10 mg/mL → 2 mg = 0.20 mL = 20 units (U-100).
ghk-cu.titration: Fixed within 1–2 mg/day SC; lowest effective dose; cap duration to limit copper accumulation.
ghk-cu.cycle_rest: Cycle 4–8 weeks. Off after the course (NOT continuous); with chronic/repeated systemic dosing, check serum copper/ceruloplasmin before re-course.
ae_serious: Copper accumulation / toxicity (Wilson disease/copper overload absolute)
Theoretical tumor promotion (pro-angiogenic — active/prior malignancy absolute)
management: On prolonged use check serum copper + ceruloplasmin, hold if rising/symptomatic
Malignancy red flag → STOP / work up
monitoring: Baseline: exclude Wilson disease / copper overload; cancer screening current
Chronic systemic use: serum copper + ceruloplasmin (± LFTs)
Wk 2 tolerance; end-of-course response; copper labs before re-course
ghk-cu.discontinuation: End of 4–8 wk course; rising/symptomatic copper; malignancy red flag; hypersensitivity; pregnancy; no benefit. No taper.

thymosin-alpha-1.status: ⚠ VERIFY — NOT FDA-approved in the US; marketed as Zadaxin in ~35 countries. Real RCTs for specific indications (chronic hep B, melanoma adjuvant, pancreatitis, colorectal), NOT for vitality/anti-aging. Indication-driven course.
thymosin-alpha-1.candidacy: Use against a defined immune problem, not a generic booster. Transplant on immunosuppression is ABSOLUTE (rejection/graft-failure hazard); severe active autoimmune disease is ABSOLUTE (flare).
thymosin-alpha-1.starting_dose: 1.6 mg SC twice weekly (conventional Zadaxin unit dose). Recon DEFAULT: 5 mg vial + 1 mL BAC → 5 mg/mL → 1.6 mg = 0.32 mL = 32 units.
thymosin-alpha-1.titration: Fixed unit dose; titrate regimen/duration to response.
thymosin-alpha-1.cycle_rest: Cycle 8–24 weeks, indication-driven. No fixed off-period; lowest effective regimen; stop when objective met.
monitoring: Baseline: exclude transplant/immunosuppression, severe active autoimmune disease; define target + indication labs
Watch autoimmune symptoms
thymosin-alpha-1.discontinuation: Objective met/unmet; autoimmune flare; transplant scenario; hypersensitivity; pregnancy. No taper.
sources: hep B NCT00291616 / NCT02366208; melanoma NCT00911443; colorectal NCT05086614

kpv.status: ⚠ VERIFY — NOT FDA-approved. Preclinical only — zero human trials. Cycled.
kpv.candidacy: Adjunct for GI/skin inflammation, not a substitute for IBD/derm work-up. Caution in transplant/immunosuppression, severe active autoimmune disease, active/recent serious infection.
kpv.starting_dose: 200–500 mcg SC daily (or oral). Recon DEFAULT: 5 mg vial + 2.5 mL BAC → 2 mg/mL → 500 mcg = 0.25 mL = 25 units.
kpv.cycle_rest: Cycle 4–8 weeks; off after course; reassess markers before re-course.
ae_serious: Immune suppression of a needed response
management: Adjunct only — don't delay IBD/derm work-up
monitoring: Baseline: define target + markers (calprotectin/CRP); exclude active infection / transplant immunosuppression / severe autoimmune
End-of-course response
kpv.discontinuation: End of course; intercurrent infection; hypersensitivity; pregnancy; no benefit. No taper.

semax.status: ⚠ VERIFY — compounded/empiric, NOT FDA-approved; zero genuine registered trials. Intranasal conventional (0.1%); SC empiric (short half-life). Cycled/pulsed.
semax.candidacy: Nootropic/neuroprotective. Best data = small non-randomized Russian acute-stroke study. Caution: lowered seizure threshold, psychiatric instability/mania, uncontrolled HTN/CVD, nasal mucosal disease.
semax.starting_dose: Intranasal ~250–600 mcg/day (typical 300), divided BID–TID; SC 300 mcg/day (250–600). SC recon DEFAULT: 10 mg vial + 2 mL BAC → 5 mg/mL → 300 mcg = 0.06 mL = 6 units.
semax.titration: 250–600 mcg/day; start low, divide higher totals.
semax.cycle_rest: Cycle 2–4 weeks, pulsed; off between pulses.
monitoring: Baseline: psychiatric/seizure history, BP, nasal exam
During pulse: BP / mood / sleep; cognitive response
semax.discontinuation: End of pulse; seizure / psychiatric destabilization; uncontrolled BP; hypersensitivity; pregnancy. No taper.

selank.status: ⚠ VERIFY — compounded/empiric, NOT FDA-approved; zero genuine registered trials. Intranasal conventional (0.15%); SC empiric. Cycled/pulsed.
selank.candidacy: Anxiolytic/anti-stress. Data = two small Russian anxiety trials vs benzodiazepines + preclinical. Caution: additive sedation with BZD/CNS depressants; not for unsupervised withdrawal management; adjunct only in significant psychiatric illness; nasal mucosal disease.
selank.starting_dose: Intranasal ~250–500 mcg/day (typical 300), divided; SC 300 mcg/day (250–500). SC recon DEFAULT: 10 mg vial + 2 mL BAC → 5 mg/mL → 300 mcg = 0.06 mL = 6 units.
selank.cycle_rest: Cycle 2–4 weeks, pulsed; off between pulses.
ae_serious: Masking of a primary psychiatric disorder
management: Reduce dose for sedation, counsel against CNS depressants/driving
Adjunct only; no unsupervised withdrawal use
monitoring: Baseline: psychiatric history, concurrent CNS depressants, nasal exam
During pulse: sedation / mood; anxiety response
selank.discontinuation: End of pulse; excessive sedation; hypersensitivity; pregnancy. No taper.

cerebrolysin.status: NOT FDA-approved (approved in EU/RU and ~other countries). Real RCT dosing (DOIs cited) but evidence MIXED. Pre-made ampoule (215.2 mg/mL) — NO reconstitution. Course-based (10–20 days); repeat 2–4×/yr.
cerebrolysin.candidacy: Post-stroke/TBI recovery or cognitive impairment (per RCT populations). Porcine brain-derived. Absolute: hypersensitivity (anaphylaxis reported), status epilepticus / uncontrolled seizures, severe renal impairment.
cerebrolysin.starting_dose: Typical 10 mL/day; range 5–30 mL/day (post-stroke/TBI ~30 mL; cognitive 5–10 mL). IM ≤5 mL; slow IV infusion (diluted in saline ~15–60 min) for larger volumes. No reconstitution (pre-made ampoule).
cerebrolysin.titration: Choose course dose by indication/acuity; fixed within course.
ae_serious: Seizure provocation (avoid in uncontrolled seizures; slow + monitor in epilepsy)
management: Seizure → STOP; avoid in status epilepticus
monitoring: Baseline: renal function, allergy/atopy, seizure history, serotonergic/MAOI meds
Per session: infusion tolerance/vitals; neuro/cognitive response over course
cerebrolysin.discontinuation: Hypersensitivity / anaphylaxis; seizure; severe renal impairment; pregnancy/lactation (VERIFY); no benefit across courses. Course ends day 10–20 by design.

dsip.status: ⚠ VERIFY — compounded/empiric, NOT FDA-approved; zero registered trials. Sparse/inconclusive data; does not reliably promote sleep. Cycled.
dsip.candidacy: Adjunct sleep support; unpredictable response. Do not mask untreated OSA / undiagnosed sleep disorder. Caution with sedatives/hypnotics/alcohol, endocrine instability (ACTH/cortisol axis).
dsip.starting_dose: Typical 200 mcg; range 100–300 mcg at bedtime. Recon DEFAULT: 5 mg vial + 2 mL BAC → 2.5 mg/mL → 200 mcg = 0.08 mL = 8 units.
dsip.cycle_rest: Cycle 4–6 weeks; off after; reassess before resuming.
ae_serious: Masking of untreated OSA / sleep disorder
management: Reduce dose for grogginess + sleep hygiene
monitoring: Baseline: sleep history, OSA screen (study if at risk), CNS depressants, endocrine status
End-of-cycle sleep-quality response
dsip.discontinuation: End of 4–6 wk cycle; no reliable benefit; hypersensitivity; pregnancy. No taper.

epitalon.status: ⚠ VERIFY — compounded/empiric, NOT FDA-approved; zero registered trials. Telomerase/longevity claims = in-vitro/animal/weak older Russian reports, no Western replication. Pulsed — short 10–20 day courses, NOT chronic; repeat 1–2×/yr.
epitalon.candidacy: Longevity/pineal-axis support; mechanism unproven and cancer-direction uncertain → precautionary malignancy screen. Short pulses only. Caution with circadian/melatonin disorders, concurrent melatonin/hormonal therapy.
epitalon.starting_dose: Typical 5–10 mg/day. Recon DEFAULT: 10 mg vial + 1 mL BAC → 10 mg/mL → 10 mg = 1.0 mL = 100 units; 5 mg = 0.5 mL = 50 units.
epitalon.cycle_rest: 10–20 day pulse (NOT chronic); off between pulses; repeat 1–2×/year.
ae_serious: Theoretical malignancy concern (telomerase/pineal, direction unproven — screen active/prior malignancy)
management: Confirm current cancer screening, red flag → STOP/work up
monitoring: Baseline: age-appropriate cancer screening current; pineal/circadian history; concurrent melatonin/hormonal therapy
Reassess before each pulse
epitalon.discontinuation: End of 10–20 day pulse; malignancy red flag; hypersensitivity; pregnancy. No taper.
sources: (A mismatched Anisimov DOI was removed in audit — VERIFY any additional citation.)

pt-141.status: FDA-APPROVED (Vyleesi, 2019) for acquired/generalized HSDD in premenopausal women. PRN — the 1/24 h + 8/month caps ARE the throttle. Male / general-libido / ED use is OFF-LABEL.
pt-141.candidacy: ON-LABEL: premenopausal women with acquired/generalized HSDD. ABSOLUTE: uncontrolled HTN and known CVD (label). Not recommended in high CV risk.
pt-141.starting_dose: LABEL fixed: 1.75 mg SC (abdomen/thigh) PRN. Vyleesi autoinjector is pre-filled 1.75 mg / 0.3 mL — no reconstitution. Compounded DEFAULT: 10 mg vial + 5 mL BAC → 2 mg/mL → 1.75 mg = 0.875 mL (~88 units) — VERIFY pharmacy fill.
pt-141.frequency: PRN, ≥45 min before activity; max 1 dose/24 h and ≤8 doses/month (label caps).
pt-141.cycle_rest: PRN/episodic; reassess at ~8 weeks. No scheduled rest — the dose caps ARE the throttle.
ae_serious: Transient BP elevation (contraindicated in uncontrolled HTN / known CVD)
Oral naltrexone-containing products interaction (may markedly lower naltrexone — not recommended)
monitoring: Baseline: BP + CV risk (gating); oral-naltrexone review; pregnancy
On therapy: BP; benefit reassessment ~8 wk; hyperpigmentation watch
pt-141.discontinuation: No benefit by ~8 wk; intolerable nausea; hyperpigmentation; uncontrolled BP/CVD; hypersensitivity; pregnancy. No taper.
sources: FDA LABEL (Vyleesi / bremelanotide, 2019; acquired/generalized HSDD premenopausal women) — 1.75 mg SC PRN, max 1/24 h & ≤8/month

kisspeptin-10.status: ⚠ VERIFY — investigational, NOT FDA-approved. Phase 1 confirms acute LH rise; no completed efficacy RCTs and no validated SC regimen (genuine data is acute IV). Cycled.
kisspeptin-10.candidacy: HPG-axis stimulation (libido/fertility-adjacent); consent as experimental. Screen out hormone-sensitive malignancy (precautionary absolute); pregnancy outside supervised protocol; caution on gonadotropins (additive stimulation).
kisspeptin-10.starting_dose: ~100 mcg SC typical; start low (~50–100 mcg). No validated range. Recon DEFAULT: 5 mg vial + 2.5 mL BAC → 2 mg/mL → 100 mcg = 0.05 mL = 5 units.
kisspeptin-10.titration: Empiric, start ~50–100 mcg; no validated titration.
kisspeptin-10.frequency: Empiric — once daily or few times weekly; NOT established.
kisspeptin-10.cycle_rest: Cycle 4–8 weeks empiric, tied to response; off-cycle between courses (not validated).
management: Coordinate with reproductive specialist if on gonadotropins
monitoring: Baseline: hormone-sensitive malignancy screen; pregnancy; reproductive context
Consider LH / sex-steroid checks
Response across cycle
kisspeptin-10.discontinuation: End of empiric cycle; no benefit; malignancy concern; hypersensitivity; pregnancy (outside supervised protocol). No taper.
sources: Investigational; Phase 1 acute LH rise only; no validated SC regimen
No specific kisspeptin DOIs in library — VERIFY any citation

gonadorelin.status: ⚠ VERIFY — compounded only; brands Factrel/Lutrepulse discontinued. Female pulsatile ovulation-induction has real evidence; male TRT-adjunct is OFF-LABEL, empiric, limited modern data — VERIFY. Pulsatile delivery is essential (continuous receptor exposure downregulates the axis).
gonadorelin.candidacy: (a) Male TRT/HPG support (empiric/off-label); (b) Female ovulation induction (evidence-backed pump). Absolute: hypersensitivity (anaphylaxis), conditions aggravated by rising gonadotropins/sex steroids (hormone-sensitive malignancy), pituitary adenoma/tumor (VERIFY).
gonadorelin.starting_dose: Male (empiric): ~100–200 mcg SC/pulse (range 100–500). Recon DEFAULT: 10 mg vial + 5 mL BAC → 2 mg/mL → 100 mcg = 0.05 mL = 5 units (smaller fills common — match pharmacy). Female ovulation induction: ~5–20 mcg IV every 60–90 min via pump (distinct indication/dosing).
gonadorelin.titration: Empiric for TRT support; ovulation induction per pump protocol. Pulsatility matters more than dose size.
gonadorelin.frequency: TRT support: EOD or 2–3×/week (some daily/twice-weekly) — these intermittent SC injections ARE the pulsatile delivery (distinct from the continuous receptor occupancy that downregulates the axis). Ovulation induction: every 60–90 min via pump.
gonadorelin.cycle_rest: TRT support continuous alongside testosterone (no scheduled rest) — but delivered as intermittent pulses, not continuous infusion. Ovulation induction per pump protocol.
ae_serious: OHSS (cyst rupture, ascites, thromboembolism — avoid in ovarian enlargement)
management: Screen out hormone-sensitive malignancy / pituitary adenoma
monitoring: Baseline: exclude hormone-sensitive malignancy / pituitary adenoma (VERIFY); pregnancy as relevant
Male: LH/FSH, total/free testosterone, semen params as indicated
Ovulation induction: follicular US + estradiol; OHSS watch
gonadorelin.discontinuation: Hypersensitivity / anaphylaxis; OHSS / ovarian enlargement; hormone-sensitive malignancy; pituitary concern; loss of benefit. TRT support tied to TRT course. No specific taper.
sources: Compounded only — VERIFY (male TRT-adjunct off-label/empiric); female pulsatile ovulation-induction has real evidence
No specific gonadorelin DOIs in library — VERIFY any citation

melanotan-ii.status: ⚠ VERIFY — NOT FDA-approved for any use; distinct from afamelanotide (SCENESSE). Gray-market; potency/purity unreliable (~30% in forensic analyses). No validated cycle. Low-start titration mandatory; melanoma screening mandatory.
melanotan-ii.candidacy: Tanning/pigmentation (off-target libido). ABSOLUTE: melanoma / any pigmented-atypical-changing lesion / personal-family melanoma history (documented melanoma incl. oral mucosal after intranasal use); uncontrolled HTN / significant CVD ABSOLUTE (hypertensive crises, renal infarction). Mandate full-body skin surveillance.
melanotan-ii.starting_dose: Reported / gray-market (NOT recommended): Loading — start LOW ~0.25 mg (250 mcg) SC daily, titrate cautiously toward ~0.5–1 mg/day as tolerated (low start limits nausea, flushing, priapism). Maintenance ~1 mg SC 1–2×/week. Recon DEFAULT: 10 mg vial + 5 mL BAC → 2 mg/mL → 250 mcg = 0.125 mL = 12.5 units; 1 mg = 0.5 mL = 50 units (purity unreliable).
melanotan-ii.titration: Low-start titration MANDATORY: begin ~250 mcg/day, increase slowly only as tolerated; step down to weekly maintenance at target pigmentation.
melanotan-ii.cycle_rest: N/A — gray-market; no validated cycle; off between loading phases.
ae_serious: Hypertensive crisis / renal infarction (absolute in uncontrolled HTN/CVD)
management: Priapism >4 h → emergency; avoid in predisposed (sickle cell, prior priapism)
Any new/changing pigmented lesion → STOP + derm/melanoma referral
Do not use in uncontrolled HTN/CVD, monitor BP
monitoring: Baseline: full-body skin/dermatologic exam (gating) + BP/CV (gating)
On therapy: interval full-body skin surveillance; BP; priapism counseling; CK if symptomatic
melanotan-ii.discontinuation: New/changing pigmented lesion; priapism; hypertensive response; rhabdomyolysis; hypersensitivity; pregnancy; patient preference. No taper.
sources: Case reports: melanoma and new/changing moles; nausea/flushing/priapism common
No specific Melanotan II DOIs in library (case-report-derived) — VERIFY any citation
